channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Amazing Digital Dentures (a failed project)
item.description: Hugging Face: Hugging Face published a post-mortem analysis of Amazing Digital Dentures, a project that did not succeed. The writeup discusses the project's failure and likely the lessons learned from the attempt.

item.title: Social intelligence Arises Between Minds - Psychology Today
item.description: Google News AI: Psychology Today article discusses how social intelligence arises through interpersonal dynamics rather than in isolation. The piece examines the mechanisms by which cognitive and emotional understanding develops between individuals in social contexts.

item.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Notion experienced a service disruption affecting access to Anthropic. Notion's head of product commented on the notable social media attention the outage received.

item.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch reports that a senior OpenAI employee claims "Chat is dead" as OpenAI works on a "super app" product. The statement reflects OpenAI's perceived shift away from chat-based interfaces as the primary interface paradigm.

item.title: The OnlyFans Economy of American AI
item.description: futurisold: A Hacker News post titled "The OnlyFans Economy of American AI" presents commentary on the current state of AI industry economics. The author uses an analogy to OnlyFans, a subscription platform, to characterize dynamics in American AI development and deployment.

item.title: The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
item.description: tavioto: The return on investment from AI coding tools differs for bootstrapped founders compared to other contexts. Cost efficiency and resource constraints shape how independent founders evaluate and use AI coding assistance.

item.title: Will artificial intelligence soon escape human control? - The Economist
item.description: Google News AI: The Economist publishes a piece questioning whether artificial intelligence will soon escape human control. This represents ongoing debate about AI alignment, safety, and the feasibility of maintaining human oversight of advanced systems.

item.title: Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
item.description: predkambrij: Predkambrij is requesting that Anthropic release an official Claude Desktop application with Linux support. Currently Claude Desktop is not officially available for Linux users.

item.title: LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
item.description: poisonfountain: A software engineer expresses concern that LLMs are eroding their career prospects and professional value. The post reflects uncertainty about adapting to AI-driven changes in software development.

item.title: Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
item.description: simianwords: A user asks whether thinking effort levels in Claude and ChatGPT are implemented via separate models or parameter tuning, and notes cache-breaking behavior when adjusting effort mid-conversation in Claude. The question probes the technical architecture behind configurable reasoning depth.

item.title: How Twitter Rewired My Brain
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej discusses how Twitter's platform design and usage patterns have affected his cognitive habits and mental frameworks. The content is a first-person reflection on social media's neurological and psychological impact.

item.title: Show HN: Lathe – Use LLMs to learn a new domain, not skip past it
item.description: devenjarvis: Lathe is a CLI tool and local web interface that uses LLMs to generate hands-on tutorials for learning technical domains. Users prompt a topic, run the local server, and work through the generated tutorial by reading and manually typing code, with features like table of contents, exercises, and sources.

item.title: Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts - The Guardian
item.description: Google News AI: The Guardian published analysis examining the AI boom through six charts illustrating billions in capital investment alongside hypothetical returns. The piece provides economic commentary on the current state of AI industry spending and projected value creation.

item.title: Automated QA and Testing with AI
item.description: Chrisszz: Automated QA and testing with AI applies machine learning and artificial intelligence to software quality assurance and testing processes. This approach automates test case generation, execution, and result analysis to improve testing efficiency and coverage.

item.title: Efficient and Training-Free Single-Image Diffusion Models
item.description: yorwba: Describes single-image diffusion models that operate efficiently without requiring model training. The approach enables diffusion-based image synthesis from a single input image through training-free optimization.

item.title: 7 Ways New Engineers Can Flourish in the Age of AI
item.description: jruohonen: An article offering advice to early-career engineers on how to succeed professionally as AI systems become increasingly prevalent. The piece provides seven practical or strategic recommendations for engineers entering the workforce during a period of rapid AI adoption.

item.title: I design with Claude more than Figma now
item.description: MrBuddyCasino: User reports preferring Claude over Figma for design work, suggesting an LLM's capabilities are shifting professional design practices. This reflects changing tool adoption in creative workflows driven by AI advances.

item.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin hosts a live AI Q&A discussion covering topics like AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session is part of regular Saturday livestreams and Wednesday YouTube uploads from the Dynamous community.

item.title: Tokenomics: Quantifying Where Tokens Are Used in Agentic Software Engineering
item.description: Anon84: A study examining how tokens are distributed and consumed across different components of agentic software engineering systems. The work quantifies token usage to understand efficiency and cost patterns in agent-based development workflows.

item.title: Top Trump artificial intelligence adviser to leave the White House - The Washington Post
item.description: Google News AI: A top artificial intelligence adviser to the Trump administration is departing from the White House. The departure is reported by The Washington Post via Google News.

item.title: Why Aren't We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?
item.description: pseudolus: The post questions why the AI industry lacks comprehensive measurement of human impact, suggesting this is a significant oversight in how AI systems are evaluated and deployed.

item.title: Is Claude Mythos Coming?
item.description: Nate Herk | AI Automation: Nate Herk discusses a leaked Mythos identifier from Anthropic's API that sparked launch speculation. He argues the leak does not signal an imminent public release and predicts Mythos capabilities will instead be quietly folded into the next Claude Opus before a standalone product launches.

item.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
item.description: TechCrunch AI: OpenAI has unveiled Lockdown Mode, a security feature designed to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks. While the feature reduces vulnerability exposure, ChatGPT may remain susceptible to prompt injections, but the goal is to minimize the likelihood of sensitive data being shared during such attacks.

item.title: Computex 2026: Are We Heading for the Agentic PC Era Yet?
item.description: rbanffy: The author poses an inquiry at Computex 2026 about whether consumer PC hardware and software is moving toward an "agentic PC" paradigm where systems autonomously execute user goals. This reflects ongoing industry debates about the applicability of AI agent frameworks to traditional personal computing.

item.title: Claude, Teach Me Something
item.description: dannyboland: A HackerNews post by dannyboland requesting Claude to teach something. The post is minimal but represents engagement with Claude's capabilities as an educational or knowledge-sharing tool.

item.title: Meta confirms 1000s of Instagram accounts were hacked by abusing its AI chatbot
item.description: speckx: Meta confirmed that thousands of Instagram accounts were compromised through exploitation of vulnerabilities in its AI chatbot. The incident highlights security risks when AI systems are integrated into authentication or account access workflows.

item.title: Law Professors Prefer AI over Peer Answers
item.description: davidbarker: Law professors evaluate AI responses more favorably than peer responses. This finding suggests that AI systems may be offering competitive or superior explanations and reasoning for legal questions.

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch predicts that Apple's WWDC 2026 conference will feature a major revamp of Siri and updates to Apple Intelligence. The article discusses anticipated improvements to Siri and the Apple Intelligence platform.

item.title: Perplexity Just Built an AI That Does Everything
item.description: Matthew Berman: Matthew Berman reviews Perplexity Computer, a new AI product from Perplexity that appears to handle multiple capabilities. The video title suggests it is marketed as a comprehensive AI system.

item.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his position as White House AI advisor. He is reportedly starting a new institution to continue influencing Trump administration AI policy.

item.title: OpenAI and the White House have competing visions for regulating artificial intelligence - Mashable
item.description: Google News AI: OpenAI and the White House hold different visions for how artificial intelligence should be regulated. The article covers the debate over regulatory approaches between the two parties.

item.title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
item.description: rsn243: A research paper proposing a unified framework connecting decision trees and diffusion models. The work explores mathematical relationships between these two architectures through the lens of flows and tree structures.

item.title: 24 Agents tasked to build a $1B app
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej documents a scenario in which 24 AI agents are tasked with building a hypothetical billion-dollar application. The video explores the practical capabilities and coordination of multiple AI agents working together on a software development project.

item.title: AI Can't Care
item.description: mooreds: A commentary titled "AI Can't Care" expresses a position on the limitations of AI systems regarding emotional or moral capability. The piece appears to argue that artificial intelligence lacks genuine capacity for care or concern, addressing a fundamental characteristic of AI cognition.

item.title: LLM Research Papers: The 2026 List (January to May)
item.description: Ahead of AI: Ahead of AI published a curated roundup of notable LLM research papers from January to May 2026. The post serves as a research digest highlighting significant work in the large language model field during the first half of the year.

item.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: maltalex: The S&P 500 index has rejected SpaceX and is blocking entry for major AI companies OpenAI and Anthropic. This reflects broader market dynamics and index composition criteria for inclusion.

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space reports a slow news day in AI. The phrase "RSI" likely refers to repetitive strain injury or a recurring market sentiment, indicating minimal significant developments.

item.title: micropython-wasm 0.1a2
item.description: Simon Willison: micropython-wasm 0.1a2 is a release adding a CLI interface to enable running MicroPython code in WebAssembly sandboxes. The project allows Python code execution in isolated, browser-compatible environments.

item.title: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison released micropython-wasm, an alpha package for running Python code in a sandbox using MicroPython compiled to WebAssembly. The sandbox prevents code from accessing unapproved files, network connections, or harmful system operations, addressing security concerns with plugin systems in Datasette, LLM, and sqlite-utils.

item.title: Ask HN: Why is the HN crowd so anti-AI?
item.description: Ekami: The author argues that Hacker News exhibits anti-AI bias, citing recurring criticism of AI-generated code quality. He counters that execution speed and shipping velocity matter more than code elegance, noting that AI-assisted development enables faster iteration, rapid feedback cycles, and version deployment compared to manual engineering practices.

item.title: OpenAI Help: Lockdown Mode
item.description: Simon Willison: OpenAI has released Lockdown Mode, a security feature rolling out across ChatGPT personal and business accounts. It limits outbound network requests to prevent data exfiltration during prompt injection attacks, addressing one leg of the "Lethal Trifecta" vulnerability without requiring AI systems to detect attacks.

item.title: Anthropic Calls for "Global AI Pause"
item.description: Wes Roth: Wes Roth reports on Anthropic's call for a global AI pause. The video covers recent developments at major AI organizations including OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, NVIDIA, and open-source projects.

item.title: AGI is Here. Anthropic Just Proved It.
item.description: Nate Herk | AI Automation: Nate Herk argues that AGI has already arrived based on Anthropic's report showing 80% of their shipped code is written by AI. The video analyzes three scenarios for what comes next and discusses why Anthropic warns about risks despite building powerful self-improving systems.

item.title: $1,300,000 in TOKENS
item.description: Matthew Berman: Matthew Berman's YouTube video discusses a large token budget of $1,300,000. The video appears to be commentary or analysis on token costs in AI systems, posted on YouTube with social media and sponsorship information provided.

item.title: Transformers are inherently succinct
item.description: brandonb: A paper accepted to ICLR 2026 presents a theoretical or empirical finding about Transformers' inherent succinctness. The work was selected as one of three outstanding papers at the top-tier conference.

item.title: How to Stop Shipping Low-Quality RL Environments (with Examples)
item.description: Latent Space: A blog post that critiques low-quality reinforcement learning environments and their negative impact on model training. The author shares observations from analyzing training trajectories and provides guidance on fixing environment harnesses.

item.title: Harness engineering: Leveraging Codex in an agent-first world
item.description: pramodbiligiri: The piece discusses harness engineering as an approach to leverage Codex in the context of agent-first systems. It examines how code generation models can be integrated into agentic workflows for improved engineering outcomes.

item.title: Launch HN: General Instinct (YC P26) – Frontier models on edge devices
item.description: guanming0717: General Instinct released InstinctRazor, a technique for compressing large mixture-of-experts models like Qwen3.5-122B into edge-deployable sizes. The approach compresses a 245 GB BF16 MoE model to 48 GiB GGUF while matching or exceeding performance of smaller models on benchmarks, with peak VRAM usage around 7.6-8 GB when running with expert streaming.

item.title: Gemma 4 QAT models: Optimizing compression for mobile and laptop efficiency
item.description: theanonymousone: Gemma 4 QAT models represent quantization-aware training optimization applied to Google's Gemma 4 language model. The approach targets efficient deployment on mobile and laptop platforms by reducing model size and computational requirements through compression.

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in May 2026
item.description: Google AI: Google AI published a recap of their AI announcements from May 2026. The post aggregates the month's latest AI updates and developments from the company.
